back_translation: In those days there was no king in Israel, and in those days the tribe of the Danites was seeking for itself an inheritance to dwell in, for until that day no inheritance had fallen to them among the tribes of Israel.

The descendants of Dan sent from their clan five men from their parts — men of valor from Zorah and from Eshtaol — to spy out the land and to explore it. They said to them, 'Go, explore the land.' They came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, and lodged there.

When they were near the house of Micah, they recognized the voice of the young Levite, and they turned aside there and said to him, 'Who brought you here? What are you doing in this place? What do you have here?'
He said to them, 'Such and such Micah has done for me. He has hired me, and I have become his priest.'
They said to him, 'Please inquire of God, that we may know whether our journey on which we are going will be successful.'
The priest said to them, 'Go in peace. Before the LORD is your journey on which you are going.'

The five men went and came to Laish, and they saw the people who were in it dwelling securely, according to the custom of the Sidonians, quiet and trusting, and no one in the land was putting them to shame in anything — possessing dominion — and they were far from the Sidonians and had no dealings with anyone.
They came to their brothers at Zorah and Eshtaol, and their brothers said to them, 'What do you say?'
They said, 'Arise, let us go up against them, for we have seen the land, and behold, it is very good. Are you keeping silent? Do not be slothful to go and enter and take possession of the land.'
'When you go, you will come to a trusting people and a broad land, for God has given it into your hand — a place where there is no lack of anything that is in the land.'
Six hundred men from the clan of the Danites set out from there, from Zorah and from Eshtaol, girded with weapons of war.
They went up and camped at Kiriath-jearim in Judah. Therefore they called that place 'Camp of Dan' until this day; behold, it is behind Kiriath-jearim.
They passed on from there to the hill country of Ephraim and came as far as the house of Micah.

The five men who had gone to spy out the land of Laish answered and said to their brothers, 'Do you know that there is in these houses an ephod and teraphim, a carved image and a molten image? Now then, consider what you should do.'
They turned aside there and came to the house of the young Levite, the house of Micah, and asked him of his welfare.
The six hundred men girded with their weapons of war, who were of the descendants of Dan, stood at the entrance of the gate.

The five men who had gone to spy out the land went up; they entered there and took the carved image, the ephod, the teraphim, and the molten image. The priest was standing at the entrance of the gate with the six hundred men girded with weapons of war.
These men entered the house of Micah and took the carved image of the ephod, the teraphim, and the molten image. The priest said to them, 'What are you doing?'

They said to him, 'Be silent! Put your hand over your mouth and come with us, and be to us a father and a priest. Is it better for you to be a priest of the house of one man, or to be a priest of a tribe and a clan in Israel?'
The heart of the priest was glad, and he took the ephod and the teraphim and the carved image, and went in among the people.
They turned and went, and they put the little children, the livestock, and the goods before them.
They had gone far from the house of Micah when the men in the houses that were near the house of Micah were called out, and they overtook the descendants of Dan.
They called out to the descendants of Dan, and they turned their faces and said to Micah, 'What is the matter with you, that you have been called out?'
He said, 'You have taken away my gods that I made, and the priest, and you have gone. What else do I have? How then do you say to me, What is the matter with you?'
The descendants of Dan said to him, 'Do not let your voice be heard with us, lest men of bitter soul fall upon you, and you lose your life and the lives of your household.'
The descendants of Dan went on their way, and Micah saw that they were too strong for him, and he turned and returned to his house.
They took what Micah had made, and the priest who was his, and came to Laish, to a quiet and trusting people, and struck them with the edge of the sword, and burned the city with fire.
There was no deliverer, because it was far from Sidon, and they had no dealings with anyone. It was in the valley belonging to Beth-rehob. They built the city and dwelt in it.
They called the name of the city 'Dan,' after the name of Dan their father who was born to Israel. But Laish was the name of the city at first.

The descendants of Dan set up for themselves the carved image, and Jonathan son of Gershom son of Moses — he and his sons — became priests for the tribe of the Danites until the day of the captivity of the land.
They set up for themselves the carved image of Micah which he had made, all the days that the house of God was at Shiloh.
